title: Housing crisis - a test for Europe's social stability? MEPs clash in The Ring
content: In this new edition of The Ring, broadcast from the European Parliament in Brussels, MEPs Dirk Gotink (EPP) and Maria Ohisalo (Greens) go face-to-face on the growing housing crisis in Europe.
snippet: In this new edition of The Ring, broadcast from the European Parliament in Brussels, MEPs Dirk Gotink (EPP) and Maria Ohisalo (Greens) go face-to-face on the growing housing crisis in Europe.

title: Ukraine needs full not symbolic membership in the EU, Zelenskyy says
content: With a €90 billion loan for Kyiv unlocked and a new package of sanctions against Russia going ahead, attention is turning to Hungary’s long-standing veto on Ukraine’s EU accession.
snippet: With a €90 billion loan for Kyiv unlocked and a new package of sanctions against Russia going ahead, attention is turning to Hungary’s long-standing veto on Ukraine’s EU accession.

title: 'Made in Europe' law should be limited to geographically close countries, leading MEP says
content: French liberal MEP Christophe Grudler, who will lead negotiations on a “Made in Europe” law in the European Parliament, told Euronews the European preference proposed by the European Commission to counter foreign competition in the EU market extends too many non-EU countries.

title: EU leaders meet in Cyprus to talk Ukraine, Hormuz, energy and mutual defence
content: One of the key issues at the summit will be Article 42.7 of the EU treaties, which provides mutual assistance in case of an armed attack. Member states want to develop a playbook to know how the article works in practice.

title: Iran's exiled crown prince Reza Pahlavi splattered with red liquid in Berlin
content: The alleged perpetrator, whose name was not released in line with German privacy rules, was immediately detained by police.
snippet: The alleged perpetrator, whose name was not released in line with German privacy rules, was immediately detained by police.

title: EU agrees fresh sanctions on Russia but leaves maritime services ban on hold
content: Due to Greece and Malta's concerns, the full ban on maritime services will be left on hold pending an agreement at the G7 level. The US, however, is going in the opposite direction by providing sanctions relief to Russia.

title: EU approves €90 billion loan for Ukraine after Hungary lifts controversial veto
content: The two-month-long impasse over the €90 billion for Ukraine came to an end on Thursday after Hungary lifted its contentious veto. Brussels says the first payment will be made "as soon as possible".
snippet: The two-month-long impasse over the €90 billion for Ukraine came to an end on Thursday after Hungary lifted its contentious veto. Brussels says the first payment will be made "as soon as possible".

title: European Economic Congress 2026: Is Europe too late to the metal recycling game?
content: As the energy transition drives demand for battery metals, European policymakers are scrambling to catch up on recycling and raw material security — decades after the rest of the world got started.
snippet: As the energy transition drives demand for battery metals, European policymakers are scrambling to catch up on recycling and raw material security — decades after the rest of the world got started.

title: 'Ground the jets' — EU lawmakers take aim at luxury flights in energy crunch
content: The call comes as Europe’s aviation sector braces for widespread disruption, with airlines warning of flight cancellations. Against this backdrop, European lawmakers argue that continued unrestricted use of private jets represents a glaring inequity.

title: European Economic Congress opens in Katowice with focus on self-sufficiency
content: In the midst of growing geopolitical and economic uncertainty for the continent, the latest iteration of the European Economic Congress began in Katowice on Wednesday with a focus on economic survival.
snippet: In the midst of growing geopolitical and economic uncertainty for the continent, the latest iteration of the European Economic Congress began in Katowice on Wednesday with a focus on economic survival

title: Watch: Why the fuel crisis might ground your summer vacation flights?
content: If you are planning a holiday this year, you might want to check your bank balance and your flight status before you pack. Europeans are entering what is shaping up to be a "Surcharge Summer". What causes this turbulence?

title: Adapt or lose: Ukraine's drone war is exposing Europe's weakness
content: Europe risks falling behind in modern warfare unless it learns from Ukraine's rapid, drone-driven innovation model, argues Dr Robert Brüll, founder and CEO of German company FibreCoat, in an opinion piece for Euronews.

title: Winning Hungary's election hasn't stopped false claims about Péter Magyar
content: Misleading posts misrepresent the Hungarian prime minister-elect by using old clips out of context.
snippet: Misleading posts misrepresent the Hungarian prime minister-elect by using old clips out of context.

title: At least 18 injured, five critically, in head-on train crash in Denmark, officials say
content: Police were unable to provide information about the cause of the accident, which occurred near a level crossing in a rural wooded area near the town of Hillerod, about 40 kilometres north of Copenhagen.
snippet: Police were unable to provide information about the cause of the accident, which occurred near a level crossing in a rural wooded area near the town of Hillerod, about 40 kilometres north of Copenhage

title: France and UK renew landmark pact to curb English Channel crossings
content: The aim of the agreement is to prevent illegal crossings of the English Channel. The two countries' interior ministers are due to meet near the northwestern French city of Dunkirk on Thursday.
snippet: The aim of the agreement is to prevent illegal crossings of the English Channel. The two countries' interior ministers are due to meet near the northwestern French city of Dunkirk on Thursday.

title: Trump attacks against NATO allies 'painful', says former NATO chief
content: Former NATO secretary general Anders Fogh Rasmussen says the hostility US President Trump holds for NATO allies is 'painful' to see. He says Europe needs to move fast to become independent from US security architecture, and, where possible, make a preference for European weapons over US-made.

title: 'Recovery could take years’: EU energy commissioner forecasts future energy pain
content: European Energy Commissioner Dan Jørgensen has warned in exclusive comments to Euronews that, even if the Middle East conflict is resolved, energy prices will not stabilise to pre-war levels for years.
snippet: European Energy Commissioner Dan Jørgensen has warned in exclusive comments to Euronews that, even if the Middle East conflict is resolved, energy prices will not stabilise to pre-war levels for years

title: Latvian defence minister has 'no doubt' US would aid NATO allies if needed
content: Latvian Defence Minister Andris Sprūds hailed an "excellent relationship" between the US and its allies despite President Donald Trump's inflammatory rhetoric and threats to exit NATO.
snippet: Latvian Defence Minister Andris Sprūds hailed an "excellent relationship" between the US and its allies despite President Donald Trump's inflammatory rhetoric and threats to exit NATO.

title: Russian oil flow to Slovakia via Druzhba pipeline resumes
content: Bratislava confirmed that the country started receiving crude oil through the Druzhba pipeline early on Thursday, after a three-month halt.
snippet: Bratislava confirmed that the country started receiving crude oil through the Druzhba pipeline early on Thursday, after a three-month halt.

title: Does lowering speed limits have an impact on road deaths and injuries in Europe?
content: From Paris to Stuttgart, cities are hitting the brakes on speed in an effort to save lives. Could this be the key to reaching zero road deaths in the EU by 2050?
snippet: From Paris to Stuttgart, cities are hitting the brakes on speed in an effort to save lives. Could this be the key to reaching zero road deaths in the EU by 2050?

title: The EU's age-verification app: a long-awaited ‘technical fix’
content: Brussels says its age-verification app is ready, but critics argue the Commission is both too slow and focused on the wrong problem. The presentation has already sparked debate over its security and effectiveness.

title: Second French peacekeeper dies after ambush blamed on Hezbollah, Macron says
content: Established in 1978, UNIFIL has more than 10,000 peacekeepers from 50 countries who patrol the Blue Line, the UN-drawn border between Lebanon and Israel.
snippet: Established in 1978, UNIFIL has more than 10,000 peacekeepers from 50 countries who patrol the Blue Line, the UN-drawn border between Lebanon and Israel.

title: Speed and deterrence: Germany's defence minister unveils first ever military strategy
content: Germany is reshaping its armed forces. The Bundeswehr is set to become faster, more capable and more technologically advanced, with the ability to strike targets precisely at longer range.
snippet: Germany is reshaping its armed forces. The Bundeswehr is set to become faster, more capable and more technologically advanced, with the ability to strike targets precisely at longer range.

title: Czech students protest government plans to cut public media funding
content: The new coalition government led by populist Prime Minister Andrej Babiš drafted the plan to scrap the fees that individuals, households and businesses pay to access the outlets.
snippet: The new coalition government led by populist Prime Minister Andrej Babiš drafted the plan to scrap the fees that individuals, households and businesses pay to access the outlets.

title: Gáspár Orbán, son of Hungary's outgoing prime minister, discharged from military
content: Orbán was sworn into the Hungarian Defence Forces in 2019 and in 2020 was sent on a nine-month training course at the elite Royal Military Academy Sandhurst in the UK.
snippet: Orbán was sworn into the Hungarian Defence Forces in 2019 and in 2020 was sent on a nine-month training course at the elite Royal Military Academy Sandhurst in the UK.

title: Exclusive: Hungary’s new government to review Orbán's EU defence plan over corruption concerns
content: Péter Magyar's team is reassessing Hungary's €16 billion SAFE defence plan submitted by the defeated Orbán government, citing corruption risks. The European Commission is open to engaging with the new administration.

title: Spain's far-right Vox returns to power in regional coalition government with Popular Party
content: During a debate in Spain's national parliament on Wednesday, Socialist Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez called the PP-Vox agreement in Extremadura "a kick to the constitution."
snippet: During a debate in Spain's national parliament on Wednesday, Socialist Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez called the PP-Vox agreement in Extremadura "a kick to the constitution."

title: A new flotilla is heading to Gaza, amid fears of Israeli interceptions
content: Around a thousand people will try to reach Gaza as part of a humanitarian mission led by civil society, which Israel has called in the past a “threat” and a “provocation”.
snippet: Around a thousand people will try to reach Gaza as part of a humanitarian mission led by civil society, which Israel has called in the past a “threat” and a “provocation”.

title: Austrian court acquits ex-official Johannes Peterlik over Novichok document leak
content: Prosecutors had accused Peterlik of requesting "without official necessity" a classified report in 2018 that included the formula for Novichok, a military-grade nerve agent developed in the Soviet Union.

title: 'Energy crisis will hit prices for months or even years,' says Commissioner Jørgensen
content: The European Commission announced on Wednesday a new 'fuel observatory' to monitor jet fuel stocks and prevent EU countries from hoarding fuel at others' expense.
snippet: The European Commission announced on Wednesday a new 'fuel observatory' to monitor jet fuel stocks and prevent EU countries from hoarding fuel at others' expense.

title: Greek parliament lifts immunity of all 13 MPs probed in EU farm subsidy scandal
content: The EU's European Public Prosecutor's Office has announced probes into 20 New Democracy members, including active and former lawmakers.

title: 'Learn from Ukraine or defence money will be wasted,' Sanna Marin warns EU

title: Deepfakes of frontline Ukrainian soldiers aim to undermine morale
content: Moscow is using AI-generated videos of Ukrainian soldiers to portray the country as weak and undermine trust in its central command.
snippet: Moscow is using AI-generated videos of Ukrainian soldiers to portray the country as weak and undermine trust in its central command.

title: Keir Starmer defies resignation calls over Mandelson envoy row

title: EU calls for energy vouchers, social tariffs and VAT reductions to curb soaring prices

title: Moldovan oligarch Vladimir Plahotniuc sentenced to 19 years in prison over $1bn fraud

title: 'I told Orbán his LGBT law was homophobic,' says Luxembourg's foreign minister
content: In an interview with Euronews' Europe Today show, Xavier Bettel said he once confronted outgoing Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán about the law he had passed targeting LGBT people. Bettel said he told Orbán: "Being gay is not a choice, but being homophobic is a choice."

title: Europe is subsidising its way into a deeper energy crisis
content: Energy crisis: Governments should replace blanket tax cuts and price caps with direct income transfers for vulnerable households, liquidity support and non-earmarked tax credits for exposed sectors, writes Judith Arnal, State Economist in Spain, in an opinion piece for Euronews.

title: Europe can't 'rule out' a war with Russia says Sanna Marin
content: Former Finnish Prime Minister Sanna Marin believes Europe is "vulnerable" without Ukraine and demands the EU to build its "own" military capabilities and increase European nuclear deterrence.

title: How Portugal shaped Canada's new submarines and fine-tuned Arctic defence
content: Portugal is the first country in recent history to operate a conventional submarine under the Arctic ice. The Navy's adaptations for this mission are being studied by Canada, which will incorporate them into its new fleet. Arpão has expanded "tactical options" for underwater combat in the far north.

title: EU countries push under-15 social media ban, Brussels presents age verification app
content: A wave of national bans is pushing the EU toward a continent-wide age limit. The European Commission says the enforcement technology is already in place. Watch the video.

title: EU trade chief heads to Washington hoping to unlock steel talks
content: EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič is travelling to Washington for the first time since the July 2025 EU-US trade deal, seeking to break the deadlock on removing 50% US tariffs on European steel and aluminium.

title: Italy summons Russian ambassador over TV presenter's insults at PM Giorgia Meloni
content: Relations between Rome and Moscow have been tense because of Meloni's strong support for Ukraine as it continues to fight off Russia's full-scale invasion.
